title: Closet Mudroom Bench with Hinged Boot Storage
summary: A pair of built-to-fit closet benches whose seats hinge up to reveal a boot storage compartment behind — sized to your own closet width, distinct from this catalog's other entryway benches.
description:
This bench is built specifically to install inside a closet opening, split into two separate bench units rather than one continuous one — both because a single shelf that long would sag, and because two smaller benches are far easier to carry into a closet than one that barely fits through the door. Measure your closet's overall width, divide by two, and subtract 1/4" for clearance before cutting the tops — this is a build-to-your-space project from the very first cut, not a fixed-dimension plan.

The defining feature is the hinged lid: the back plywood panel is held down 3/4" from the top specifically to leave clearance for the seat to swing open, revealing a boot storage compartment underneath rather than the fixed cubby-behind-the-seat layout this catalog's other shoe bench uses. Add optional vertical dividers before the shelves if you want separated compartments, notching the divider's top-back corner with a jigsaw to clear the top 1x3 support.

Once both benches are built, installation is its own small project: place the benches at the front of the closet and screw them together, mount a 2x2 cleat on the back wall at the same height as the plywood, then measure the remaining gap between the benches and the back wall and cut a fitted panel to hinge across that gap — closing the loop between the two separate boxes and the wall behind them.

materials.name: 1x12, cut to closet width plus 18 in per leg
1/4" plywood, for bench backs and fitted gap panel
Self-tapping screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Piano hinge or hinges, per bench
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1x12, cut to closet width plus 18 in per leg
cutList.note: Half your closet width minus 1/4", per bench — measure before cutting.

cutList.part: Bench sides and bottom shelf (per bench)
cutList.material: 1x12, cut to closet width plus 18 in per leg
1/4" plywood, for bench backs and fitted gap panel
cutList.note: Held down 3/4" from the top to clear the hinged lid.

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood, for bench backs and fitted gap panel
cutList.note: Measure the actual gap between the benches and the back wall before cutting.

steps.title: Measure and cut the tops
steps.body: If building two benches for a closet, measure the overall closet width, divide by two, and subtract 1/4" for clearance. Cut the tops (and matching side/shelf lengths) to this measurement.
materials: 1x12, cut to closet width plus 18 in per leg

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Cut two 1x3s to the same length as the bottom shelf. Attach one inset at the back top of the cabinet and one inset at the front bottom, with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, screws, or pocket holes.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Attach the back plywood panel, held down 3/4" from the top to allow clearance for the hinged lid. Nail to the sides and the top 1x3 with 3/4" nails.
materials: 1/4" plywood, for bench backs and fitted gap panel

steps.title: Add shelves and dividers
steps.body: Add any additional shelves and vertical dividers with pocket holes, screws, or nails. If adding dividers, notch the top-back corner with a jigsaw to clear the top 1x3, and install dividers before the shelves.
materials: 1x12, cut to closet width plus 18 in per leg

steps.title: Install in the closet
steps.body: Place the two benches at the front of the closet and screw them together. Mount the 2x2 cleat on the back closet wall at the same height as the plywood back panels.

steps.title: Fit and hinge the gap panel
steps.body: Measure the gap between the back of the benches and the closet's back wall. Cut plywood to fit and hinge it in place across that gap. Sand and finish the whole installation.
materials: 1/4" plywood, for bench backs and fitted gap panel
Piano hinge or hinges, per bench
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Closet mudroom bench with a hinged lid revealing boot storage
